methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantWare is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Matthijs Rijlaarsdam and Alessandro Bruno. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers—3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion—though the narrow peer set constrains methodology applicability.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using the company's $27M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions yields $54M. Peer-median valuation is not applicable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, only 1 falls into adjacent-stage or similar-tier buckets, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for reliable median pricing.

The triangulated analysis, supported by a single method, indicates a valuation range of $46M–$62M with a median estimate of $54M. Material uncertainty remains around peer comparables—the quantum computing sector's limited same-stage private market data constrains calibration—and around timing and stage-specific dynamics for frontier-tech Series A financings. The range reflects typical step-up expectations but should be stress-tested against any disclosed metrics on technical progress, IP position, or customer traction.
